overview.title: What are Webhooks
overview.description: Webhooks are HTTP callbacks that your application registers to receive automatic notifications when events occur on the platform.

items.title: Real-time
items.text: Receive events immediately, no polling required.

items.title: Auto Retry
items.text: Exponential backoff retry for guaranteed delivery.

items.title: Event Logs
items.text: Complete event history in the partner dashboard.

sections.title: Recommended for Production
sections.content: Webhooks are significantly more efficient than polling. In production, **always prefer webhooks** for receiving new orders.

setup.title: Setup Guide
setup.description: Follow this guide to register and verify your webhook URL.

items.title: Prepare your Endpoint
items.text: Create an HTTPS endpoint. Must accept `POST`, respond `200 OK` in **5 seconds**, be publicly accessible.

items.title: Register the Webhook
items.text: Register your URL via `POST /v2/webhooks`. Specify the events you want.

items.title: Challenge Verification
items.text: We send a `GET` with `?challenge=token`. Your server must return the token value.

items.title: Validate Signatures
items.text: For each received event, validate the HMAC-SHA256 signature in `X-Webhook-Signature`.

items.title: Respond 200
items.text: Always respond `HTTP 200 OK` immediately. Process the payload asynchronously.

sections.title: Webhook Handler (Node.js)

security.title: Security & Signatures
security.description: How to verify the authenticity of received webhook events.

sections.title: Never Skip Signature Validation
sections.content: Always validate `X-Webhook-Signature` before processing any event. Skipping this leaves your system vulnerable to event injection attacks.

rows.name: X-Webhook-EventID
rows.description: Unique event ID. Use for idempotency checks.

rows.name: X-Webhook-Timestamp
rows.description: Unix timestamp of when the event was generated.

retry-policy.title: Retry Policy
retry-policy.description: How the system handles webhook delivery failures.

items.title: 5th Retry — 24 hours
items.text: Final attempt. Event is discarded and logged after this.

order-created.title: Event: order.created
order-created.description: Triggered when a new order is created and awaiting partner confirmation.

sections.title: Immediate Action Required
sections.content: Confirm the order within **5 minutes** via `POST /v2/orders/{id}/confirm`. Otherwise the order is automatically cancelled.

order-cancelled.title: Event: order.cancelled
order-cancelled.description: Triggered when an order is cancelled.

order-delivered.title: Event: order.delivered
order-delivered.description: Triggered when order delivery is successfully confirmed.

order-expired.title: Event: order.expired
order-expired.description: Triggered when a PENDING order expires due to confirmation timeout.

sections.title: Monitor This Event
sections.content: High expiration rates may result in partner penalties. Monitor this event closely and optimize your confirmation flow.

register-webhook.title: Register Webhook
register-webhook.description: Registers a new webhook URL to receive platform events.

rows.name: url
rows.description: HTTPS URL of your webhook endpoint.

rows.name: events
rows.description: List of events to subscribe to. Use `["*"]` for all events.

list-webhooks.title: List Webhooks
list-webhooks.description: Returns all webhooks registered by the authenticated partner.

test-webhook.title: Test Webhook
test-webhook.description: Sends a test event to verify your endpoint is configured correctly.
